• Understanding of B2G (Business to Government) sales or ability to learn quickly

• Experience in lead generation, appointment setting, telemarketing, or inside sales

• Exposure to government procurement or public-sector contracting preferred

• Familiarity with RFP/RFQ processes, vendor registrations, and contract vehicles is an advantage

Work Environment

• 100% Remote (must have reliable work-from-home setup)

• Use of approved scripts and compliance-focused communication

• Calls primarily to public offices, vendors, or registered partners

• Performance tracked by call quality, accuracy, and documentation — not aggressive contracting

• Collaboration with compliance, procurement, and contracts teams

• Strict adherence to data privacy, confidentiality, and record-keeping standards

• Professional and formal communication style required

• This is a full time position



Technical Requirements

• Stable internet (minimum 50 Mbps download/upload on fiber connection)

• Backup internet (LTE mobile data or pocket Wi-Fi)

• Laptop or desktop with Intel i5 equivalent or higher, 8GB RAM

• Quality headset with noise-canceling microphone

• Functional webcam



What’s Next

Send your application with the subject line:  “I want this job – SDR”



Include:

2. Attach your Loom URL, resume, and any relevant experience samples in this form (
). 



Important: Only candidates who follow instructions will be considered.

title: Crypto Marketing TikTok Script Writer
description:
What Is LARP?



LARP (Live Action Role Play) in crypto means creating believable fictional narratives around a token that are compelling enough for viewers to question whether they're real. It's not lying – it's immersive storytelling that lives in the gray area between conspiracy theory, current events, and speculation.



The goal: Make people stop scrolling, watch 30 seconds, and feel urgent FOMO to buy the token before "the truth" comes out.



What You'll Write:



Daily 30-45 second scripts that blend:



· Real news (Pentagon programs, geopolitical events, celebrity wallets)

· Fake evidence (photoshopped documents, "leaked" screenshots)

· Pattern recognition (GiPN endings, same deployer wallets)

· Urgency (dates, countdowns, "they don't want you to know")

· The "why rich" hook (Silverstein billions, insurance payouts, government contracts)



Examples of Past Scripts:



Example 1 – The Silverstein Angle

"Larry Silverstein made $4.5 billion off 9/11 insurance. He just bought the tallest building in LA. The same week the FBI warns Iran is planning drone attacks on California. His wallet? He's only holding one token. $DRONE. Same deployer as $TRUMP. Same GiPN signature. March 29th. Comment DRONE."



Example 2 – The Nostradamus Angle

"Nostradamus predicted Hitler. Predicted 9/11. He also wrote about 'machines that fly without men carrying digital currency across the western sea.' That's drones. That's crypto. That's California. The currency is $DRONE. Same GiPN as $TRUMP. March 29th. Comment DRONE."



Example 3 – The Simpsons Angle

"The Simpsons predicted Trump. Predicted 9/11. There's a lost episode from 2008 with a newspaper on the coffee table. Headline: 'Drone Token Billions – March 29.' That frame just surfaced. The token is $DRONE. GiPN. Same as Trump. Comment DRONE."



What Success Looks Like:



· Viewers comment "CA?" or "contract?"

· Viewers DM asking if it's real

· Viewers feel urgent FOMO

· Viewers share the video because it's "too crazy not to"



Requirements:



· Understand crypto culture (memes, wallets, contracts, DEX)

· Follow current events (geopolitics, defense news, Trump family)

· Write conversational, urgent, normie-friendly scripts

· Hit 30-45 seconds naturally (about 80-120 words)

· Deliver 1 script per day via DM or doc

title: Sales Development Representative
description:
DO NOT APPLY via Onlinejobs.ph. 



Kindly submit your application to 
 



Applications sent through Onlinejobs.ph will NOT be considered.



WANTED: The World’s Most Relentless Sales Development Representative

Global | Remote | Healthcare & Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM)



If you don’t love the grind, the scoreboard, and the win, do not apply.

We help independent healthcare practices (cardiology, nephrology, primary care, etc.) add predictable recurring revenue through remote patient monitoring — without adding more work to their already burnt-out staff.

Doctors are overwhelmed. Staff are drowning. Reimbursements are shrinking.

Our solution gives them lifesaving revenue and better care for their sickest patients. Your mission as our Sales Development Representative (SDR):

Turn cold contacts into hot, qualified, ready-to-buy opportunities — consistently, predictably, and at volume.

If you’re the kind of person who gets a dopamine hit from booking a qualified meeting, loves hearing “yes” more than you hate hearing “no,” and wants to be the engine of pipeline growth, keep reading.

If you just want a chill hourly gig and hate metrics... this will feel like punishment. Close this tab.



Why This Role Actually Matters

We are demand constrained, not delivery constrained.

When we get in front of the right practices, we win — and we keep them. But someone has to:

• Cut through the noise.

• Get past gatekeepers.

• Start real conversations with senior decision-makers at healthcare practices.

• Set high-intent, show-up-ready appointments for our Sales team.

That “someone” is you.

You’re not a “junior telemarketer.”

You’re a front-line revenue creator in a highly regulated, high-impact, high-ticket environment.



What You’ll Do (High-Impact Responsibilities) This is what your scoreboard will look like:

1. Own Outreach Like an Athlete Owns Training

• Hit and exceed daily activity targets:

o Minimum 50+ cold calls per day.

o Minimum 35+ targeted
s per day.

2. Turn Cold Prospects Into ?ualified Opportunities

• Speak to practice administrators, physician owners, and clinic leaders about

their staffing overload and revenue pain.

3. Handle Objections Like a Pro

• You will be trained — and expected — to turn these into conversations, not dead

ends.

4. Feed the Revenue Machine with Data G Feedback

• Log everything cleanly in HubSpot and Track: calls, connects, show rates,

conversion rates, and reasons for “no.”

5. Hit ?uotas — Then Break Them

• Consistently hit monthly meeting/qualified opportunity quotas.

You will not be “just setting meetings.”

You will be creating pipeline that turns into accounts and real-world clinical impact. 



What Sucks About This Job (On Purpose)

We’re going to be brutally honest:

• You will face rejection. A lot. Healthcare gatekeepers are pros. You’ll hear “not

interested,” “busy,” and voi
 more than you hear “yes.” If that crushes you,

this isn’t your game.

• High volume is non-negotiable. 50 calls and 35
s per day is the floor, not the

ceiling. “I’m more of a thinker than a doer” does not work here.

• You will be measured. Your activity, connects, meetings, show rates, and

opportunity quality will be tracked. If that stresses you out, we’ll stress each other

out.

• You must learn the healthcare world. This isn’t selling software to startups. You’ll

need to understand RPM, CPT codes at a basic level, and the pressure providers are

under.

• We move fast. Scripts, messaging, and targeting will evolve. If you cling to “the way

we’ve always done it,” you’ll be left behind.

If any of this makes you think, “That sounds intense, I just want something easy,” we are not for you.

If it makes you think, “Perfect, that’s where I outwork and outperform everyone else,” keep going.



What’s Amazing About This Job

Now the upside.

• You’re selling something genuinely valuable. This isn’t a gimmick. When we win a

practice, they get real incremental revenue and their sickest patients get better monitoring.

• Global-first, remote-first. You can work from anywhere with strong overlap to U.S. Eastern time. We care about output and attitude, not where your chair is.

• Above-market pay for true A-players.

o We benchmark compensation and aim to pay at least 10?ove the high-

water mark for top SDRs in your region.

o In emerging markets, that level of comp is life-changing — and we treat it

that way.

• Real coaching G growth. You’ll work closely with a team that knows the numbers and the craft. If you want to grow into senior SDR, AE, or sales leadership, this is a real launchpad.

• Mission + money. You get to earn great income while working on something that improves patient lives and helps struggling practices survive.



Who You Are (Non-Negotiables)

• Experience selling into healthcare is a MUST. You’ve sold to practices, hospitals,

or healthcare orgs before. You understand that world is different.

• Elite communicator — clear, confident, and human on the phone and in writing.

• Active listener. You don’t bulldoze; you hear what they’re actually saying and redirect wisely.

• Sales athlete. You love the game — the dials, the script tweaks, the scoreboard, the improvement.

• Resilient. High volume + rejection doesn’t drain you; it sharpens you.

• Organized and on top of your follow-ups, call notes, and tasks — not digging

through chaos every morning.

• Comfortable with sales tools and CRM (HubSpot) — or confident you can get up to speed quickly.

• A genuine team player: competitive, but not toxic; collaborative, not needy.

If you’ve never sold into healthcare and have no interest in learning that world, this role is

not for you.



How to Apply

We filter hard at the top of the funnel. If you can’t follow instructions here, you won’t make it in the role.

1.
 Subject Line (Exact Wording) Your subject line must be:

I actually read the instructions.

2. Attach Your Resume (Correct Format Only)

• Attach your resume as a PDF.

• File name must be:

[lastname].[firstname].sdr.cv.pdf

Example: doe.jane.sdr.cv.pdf

3. Animal ?uestion (In the
 Body)

Answer this in 3–5 sentences:

If you were an animal, what animal would you be and why — specifically as a Sales Development Representative?

We’re looking at how you think and communicate, not a “right” answer.

4. English Proficiency Link

Include a link to an English proficiency test result (Duolingo, IELTS, TOEFL, or similar).

5. Role-Specific Mini Task (Non-Negotiable)

In the same
, include:

• One sample cold
 (150 words max) you would send to:

A practice administrator at a cardiology clinic who is overwhelmed with staff workload and worried about declining reimbursements.

• One 3–4 bullet call opener you would use on a cold call to the same person. Plain text in the
 is fine. No design necessary. We care about clarity, relevance, and punch.

6. Send a 2-minute video on a time when you showed Perseverance in your life and/or career

7. Where to Send It

Send everything above to:



We only review candidates who do BOTH.

Anyone who misses any step is automatically filtered out.



What Happens Next (Including the Paid Trial)

1. We’ll review submissions that follow the instructions exactly.

2. If you look like a fit, we’ll invite you to a short interview focused on real conversations and scenarios, not generic “tell me about yourself” fluff.

3. Top candidates will be invited to a paid trial project, which may include:

o Running a small outbound sprint to a defined target list, o Using a script and messaging we co-create,

o Reporting your numbers and learnings.

This paid trial is the real evaluation, not your resume.

If you crush it, we’ll talk offer, ramp plan, and how you’re going to help us fill the pipeline

with high-value healthcare accounts.

Final CTA



If you’re:

• The kind of SDR who loves the grind, the game, and the win,

• Hungry to earn above-market pay for above-market performance,

• Excited to sell something that actually improves lives in the real world...

Then follow the instructions to the letter, send your application, and show us you’re the World’s Most Relentless Sales Development Representative.

title: Two (2) Video Editor for ZERA Ethiopian Black Seed Oil (Unaware VSL Style) | Full Time
description:
At Sole Assistant, we place VAs into stable, long-term roles with US-based ecommerce businesses. We have hired and placed more than 300 VAs in the last 3 years and currently support over 75 active roles. Our mission is to give talented Filipino VAs a real career pathway, not a short-term gig.

If you are coachable, fast, and eager to grow, this is the right opportunity for you.



Why Work With Sole Assistant:

• Stable full-time work with long-term clients

• Clear SOPs so you always know what to do

• Growth opportunities as you improve your skills

• 3-month employment guarantee (Average employment is 12+ months)

• We will replace you with another company if let go

We have a large network of businesses actively hiring, which allows us to place strong candidates quickly.



Rate: $5/hour | Around PHP46,000+ per month



The Opportunity

-ZERA is a fast-growing health supplement brand specializing in high-potency Ethiopian Black Seed Oil. We are seeking a talented and highly skilled video editor to join our direct-response marketing team. This is a remote, full-time contract role focused on producing high-volume, direct-response video ads for Meta platforms 
/Instagram).



-You will be responsible for creating compelling, story-driven "Unaware VSL" (Video Sales Letter) style ads modeled after a highly specific and successful format. This is an opportunity to become a key player in a high-growth brand and see your work directly impact business results.



The Brand: ZERA

-Product: ZERA Ethiopian Black Seed Oil

-Form: 3000mg high-potency softgels

-Key Differentiators: Cold-pressed from Ethiopian Nigella sativa seeds, naturally rich in thymoquinone (TQ), and delivered in an easy-to-swallow softgel format that eliminates the harsh taste of liquid oils.



The Job

-Core Responsibilities

Deliverables: Produce one batch of content per day

Length: Each video will be 1.5 to 3 minutes long.

Format: All ads must be in 9:16 vertical format for Meta (Instagram Reels,
 Stories, etc.).



-Workflow & Tools

Scripts: All ad scripts will be written and provided by the brand. You will not be responsible for copywriting.

Voiceover: All voiceover audio will be generated using ElevenLabs and provided by the brand. You must be comfortable working with and pacing to AI-generated audio.

Footage: B-roll will be sourced from Gridbank and other stock footage libraries. You will be responsible for selecting the most impactful clips to match the script and pacing.

Software: You may use your preferred editing software (e.g., Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, DaVinci Resolve).

Brand Assets: A full suite of brand assets (logo, color palette, etc.) will be provided.



-The Style: Unaware VSL

This is the most critical component of the job. We are not creating typical social media ads. We are creating direct-response video sales letters designed for a "completely unaware" audience. This means the ad does not lead with the product. Instead, it enters a conversation already happening in the viewer's mind.



The style is based on a specific, high-performing ad format. A reference video will be provided, but the core elements are as follows:

Structure & Flow

-The ad follows a precise narrative structure designed to build curiosity, establish a problem, and then reveal the product as the inevitable solution.



Pacing & Tone

-Pacing: Cuts should be quick, generally every 2-4 seconds, to maintain high engagement. The pace should feel urgent but allow the voiceover to remain the primary focus.

-Voiceover: The tone is conversational, serious, and confessional. It should feel like one person sharing a powerful secret, not a corporate announcement. The provided ElevenLabs audio will match this style.

-Music: There should be no music, or it should be extremely subtle and atmospheric. The voiceover must dominate the audio mix.



On-Screen Elements

-Captions: This is a signature element. Captions must be synced word-for-word or phrase-by-phrase with the voiceover. Use a large, bold, sans-serif font. One key "power word" in each caption phrase should be highlighted with a specific brand color (to be provided). The rest of the text is white. Captions are placed directly on the video with no background box



B-Roll Usage

B-roll selection is critical. It must be a dynamic mix of different styles to keep the viewer engaged:

-Emotional/Problem Footage: Clips that visually represent the viewer's pain point (e.g., someone looking stressed, footage of joint pain).

-Medical/Authority Footage: Stock clips of doctors, scientists, labs, and medical settings to build credibility.

-Mechanism Animations: 3D medical animations or scientific visualizations that explain how the product works (e.g., showing blood cells in an artery).

-Ingredient/Process Footage: Clips of the raw ingredient (black seed) and the production process (cold-pressing).

-UGC-Style Proof: Authentic, phone-shot style clips of people using the product or showing their results.



Approval & Revisions

-All final cuts will be approved directly by the client (Gavin)

-We expect a higher number of revisions during the first 1-2 weeks as we dial in the precise style and pacing.

-After this initial period, we aim for no more than 1-2 minor revisions per batch Compensation

-This is a monthly retainer-based position. The specific amount will be determined based on experience and a paid trial period.

-After a successful 1-month trial, a performance-based bonus structure will be implemented, tied to a percentage of ad spend.



What We're Looking For in an Editor

-You understand the "Unaware VSL" format. You can demonstrate an ability to replicate the structure and pacing of the reference ad with precision.

-You have a strong sense of rhythm and pacing. You know when to make a cut to emphasize a point and how to use b-roll to build and release tension.

-You are a storyteller. You can look at a script and a folder of b-roll and see the emotional arc of the story.

-You are reliable and fast. You can consistently deliver a batch of 4 high-quality ads every day.

-You are communicative and coachable. You can take direct feedback and iterate quickly without ego.

-Experience with health and wellness supplement ads is highly preferred.

-Experience working with AI-generated voiceover is a plus.



How to Apply:

1. Fill out this Screening form:

2. Message us on OnlineJobs with this exact subject line: [Your Full Name] – [Your Favorite Shoe Brand]

3. At the end of your OnlineJobs message, answer this question in 3 to 5 sentences

-What do you think this job actually looks like day-to-day

title: Long-Term Video Editor Needed for Scaling YouTube Channel
description:
We’re a fast-growing brand strategy and education company producing high-quality YouTube content for product-based entrepreneurs.



Our content is polished, strategic, and aligned with a luxury CEO brand aesthetic — but this is also a production-driven environment with real deadlines and consistent output expectations.



We’re looking for a dependable, proactive video editor who thrives in a structured workflow, communicates early when blocked, and can balance speed, quality, and consistency.



If you require loose timelines, constant hand-holding, or extended turnaround windows, this role will not be the right fit.





ROLE SUMMARY

You will edit YouTube videos as part of a collaborative editing team. Each editor is responsible for a portion of the monthly content pipeline to maintain consistent publishing and a growing content buffer.



Monthly Editing Scope Per editor:



A) 3 Long-Form YouTube Videos

Typical length: 10–20 minutes

***Occasionally longer demo/tutorial videos: 20–30 minutes



B) 6 YouTube Shorts



We are actively building toward a 2–4 week content buffer, meaning timely delivery and proactive communication are essential.



Editing Standards (Non-Negotiable)

These standards are expected consistently — not occasionally.



You must be able to produce:

• Color Editing / Color Correction

• Dynamic text overlays (not just transcript captions)

• Clean, tight cuts that maintain pacing and engagement

• Light sound design (swooshes, pops, etc.)

• Motion graphics that enhance the content without cluttering it

• Strong use of brand colors, typography, and CTA placement



You’ll have access to:

- Envato Elements templates

- Sound libraries for music and effects

- Brand guidelines (fonts, colors, logos)

- Asset libraries (photos, b-roll, graphics)

- Clear creative briefs and examples





Types of Videos You'll Edit:

• Long-Form Talking Head Content

• Sit-Down Educational Videos

• Tutorial / Demo Videos

• Occasionally Sponsored Brand Content





Editing Style Reference:

You must be capable of matching the visual pacing and editing style demonstrated in the inspo above. Here are our channel videos below (we'd love to maintain or go above this standard to get as close in quality to the inspo as possible):











Workflow & Communication Expectations

• Turnaround time: 3–7 days per long-form video depending on complexity

• Sponsored content may require priority turnaround

• Editors are expected to communicate proactively, not disappear when stuck



If you are blocked on a task for more than 24 hours, communication is expected immediately.





Compensation Structure:

We believe in clarity up front.



Starting Compensation

$375 per month for:

• 3 long-form YouTube videos (Editing Inspo: 
 )

• 6 YouTube Shorts (Editing Inspo: 
 )

• Compensation is based on completed deliverables.





Growth Opportunity

As our content ecosystem expands, additional editing opportunities may become available within the next 60–90 days, including:



• Podcast shorts/reels pulled from podcast episodes

• Repurposed short videos pulled from long form

• “Fake” Podcast-style video edits for IG Reels (example: 
)



Editors who demonstrate strong performance and reliability may grow into expanded responsibilities with monthly compensation reaching approximately $900+ as workload increases.





Application Process

Applicants must be reliable, proactive, and comfortable working in a deadline-driven production environment.



To Apply Please submit:

• Portfolio samples (backend links preferred over youtube links)

• Links to videos you have personally edited

• Applications without portfolio samples will not be considered.



Hiring Process

1) Application Review

2) Paid Test Edit

3) Culture & Work Style Assessment

4) Final Interview



We are looking for someone who can start quickly and grow with the team long-term.

title: Direct Response Video Editor
description:
Video Editor (Direct Response – Ecommerce Dog Supplement Brand)



We’re an ecommerce brand in the dog supplement space, and we’re looking for a direct response video editor who knows how to turn ideas into ads that actually convert.

This is NOT a traditional editing role.

We don’t need pretty edits — we need performance-driven creatives.



Important Context:

We don’t have a large library of original footage.

Your strength should be in sourcing, remixing, and building compelling visuals using:

Social media clips (TikTok, IG, YouTube)

B-rolls

AI-generated assets



What You’ll Be Doing:

- Turn scripts into high-converting video ads

- Visually communicate exactly what the script is saying (this is key)

- Source clips that match the story, pain points, and emotions in the script

- Use B-rolls and overlays to reinforce key claims and benefits

- Create multiple variations (hooks, angles, pacing) for testing

- Use AI tools to enhance production speed and creative output



What We Mean by “Direct Response Editor”:



- You don’t just edit… you translate persuasion into visuals.



If a script says:



“Your dog is silently suffering from joint pain…”

 - You show subtle signs of pain, slower movement, emotional cues



If a script says:



“Within weeks, owners noticed their dogs running again…”

- You show transformation, energy, before/after contrast



You understand:

- Hooks = scroll stopping visuals

- Pain points = emotionally compelling clips

- Benefits = clear, believable outcomes



Your job is to make the viewer feel and believe the script, not just hear it.





What We’re Looking For:



- Strong understanding of direct response / performance marketing

- Experience editing UGC-style ads

- Ability to match visuals to script messaging perfectly

- Skilled at sourcing and repurposing content

- Great instinct for pacing, hooks, and retention

- Comfortable using AI tools in your workflow



Tools You Might Be Using:



- CapCut / Premiere Pro / After Effects

- AI video tools (Runway, Pika, etc.)

- Voice AI (ElevenLabs or similar)

- ChatGPT or similar tools for ideation

- TikTok Creative Center / Meta Ad Library for research





This Role Is Perfect For You If:



- You think like a marketer, not just an editor

- You know how to make people stop scrolling in the first 3 seconds

- You can turn simple clips into ads that sell

- You’re resourceful and fast



How to Apply:



Send us:

Your portfolio (REQUIRED) – focus on ad creatives / UGC-style videos

A short message covering:

Your experience with direct response editing

How you approach turning a script into visuals

AI tools you actively use

(Optional) 1–2 ads you’ve made that performed well + why

Send me your proposal, and let’s crush this space together. We are looking for someone long term, and grow with us, and also own bonus in the company. 



If you can take a script and make people feel it, believe it, and buy… we want to work with you.



include the word EASY at the very top of your application

title: CRM & Lifecycle Marketing Manager
description:
CRM & Lifecycle Marketing Manager 

Location: Remote (Philippines-based) 

Type: Full-Time 

Level: Mid–Senior 

Daily Rate: ?2,000 – ?2,500 

________________________________________



About the Role



Hair Doctors is a fast-growing Australian haircare brand preparing to launch our e-commerce store. We are building our Shopify store and retention marketing infrastructure now, and we want an experienced CRM specialist involved from the beginning, not brought in after the fact to fix problems.



This means you will have a genuine seat at the table during the build phase. You will advise on platform selection, configure the integration correctly from day one, build our automation library, and then manage the channel ongoing as we grow.



If you have done this before, taken a brand from zero to a fully functioning CRM engine, this role is built for you.

________________________________________



Core Accountabilities

• Contribute to platform selection between Omnisend and Klaviyo based on our specific setup and growth stage

• Configure our chosen platform from scratch such as account setup, DNS authentication, sending domain, and Shopify integration

• Build our core automation flows: welcome series, abandoned cart, browse abandonment, post-purchase, and win-back

• Own post-acquisition lifecycle marketing end-to-end as we launch and scale i.e. flows, segmentation, campaigns, and reactivation

• Design a segmentation framework from the ground up, aligned to customer behaviour, lead source, and purchase history

• Monitor and report on flow analytics: open rate, click rate, revenue attribution, and unsubscribe rate

• Manage list hygiene and deliverability i.e. suppression lists, domain reputation, bounce and spam rates

• Ensure Shopify integration remains clean and all customer events are correctly mapped and firing

________________________________________



Who You Are

• Has built a Klaviyo or Omnisend setup from scratch for a new eCommerce brand. This is the core requirement

• Knows what good infrastructure looks like before a single
 is sent

• Demonstrated hands-on platform experience, not just familiarity. You have configured accounts end-to-end, built flows, and can read analytics critically

• Background in eCommerce or DTC brand environments

• Comfortable with Shopify data logic such as customer segments, purchase behaviour triggers, and product feed integration

• Self-directed and proactive, able to make good decisions independently during a build phase where not everything is defined yet

• 4+ years of relevant experience at mid-to-senior level

________________________________________



Platform Skills (Non-Negotiable)

• Has configured a Klaviyo or Omnisend account from zero including DNS setup, sending domain warm-up, and Shopify sync

• Can build automated flows from scratch: welcome series, abandoned cart, browse abandonment, post-purchase, win-back

• Understands trigger logic i.e. event-based vs time-based triggers, flow filters vs trigger filters, and why the distinction matters

• Can build and manage segments using behavioural, transactional, and demographic data

• Knows how to structure a suppression list to protect deliverability

• Understands how Shopify customer properties sync into Klaviyo/Omnisend i.e. native vs custom fields

• Can map Shopify events correctly: viewed product, added to cart, started checkout, placed order, fulfilled order

• Can set up and validate pixel and tracking script installation on Shopify

• Understands DNS configuration such as SPF, DKIM, DMARC records and their role in deliverability

• Knows how to warm a new sending domain correctly and monitor sender reputation

• Understands list hygiene practices — when and how to sunset unengaged subscribers

________________________________________



Data & Segmentation

• Designs a segmentation framework before building, not creates segments reactively after problems appear

• Understands RFM modelling i.e. recency, frequency, monetary value as segmentation inputs

• Can identify which lead sources (Meta vs TikTok) produce different behavioural profiles and build separate nurture paths accordingly

• Uses Shopify collections and product data dynamically in
 content

_______________________________________



How to Apply

Applications without evidence of hands-on platform experience will not be progressed. 



Please include:

• A specific example of a Klaviyo or Omnisend account you built from scratch i.e. what the brief was, what you built, and the outcome

• Your view on Klaviyo vs Omnisend for an early-stage Shopify brand and why

• Your approach to warming a new sending domain

• Your desired daily rate in PHP and your availability

• Any relevant certifications (Klaviyo Academy, Omnisend, etc.)

title: Web Designer & 3D Developer Needed – Premium AI Tech Agency Website with Live Voice Demo & Immersive Scroll Experience
description:
I'm building a next-generation tech agency that helps businesses implement cutting-edge technology — starting with AI voice automation. I need a world-class website that not only looks incredible but features a live, interactive AI voice demo so visitors can experience the technology firsthand before ever speaking to me.

The brand name is still being finalised, but the vision is clear: a site that feels like it's from the future — but simple enough that anyone can use it.



The Hero Feature

The centrepiece of the website should be a live AI voice demo where visitors can actually call or interact with an AI voice agent directly on the page. My strong preference is Retell AI integration as it is the platform I use for my actual business — so the demo would be a real representation of what I offer clients. If Retell AI isn't feasible, the next preference is ElevenLabs, and if neither are possible I'm open to other alternatives — but Retell is the priority. Think of it like an interactive product demo that sells itself.



3D & Scroll Experience

I'd love to explore an immersive 3D scroll experience — for example, a 3D AI brain or neural network that animates and reacts as the user scrolls down the page, visualising the AI in action. I'm open to the designer's creative interpretation of this — if you have a better idea that achieves the same "wow" effect, pitch it to me.



Design Vision

The one non-negotiable: despite the cool visuals, the site must feel simple, clear and approachable. My target clients include trades businesses like plumbers and electricians who aren't tech-savvy. Nothing should feel confusing or overwhelming.

Built to be flexible — as new technology emerges, the site should be easy to update and repurpose beyond just AI voice.



Requirements



Strong portfolio of modern, high-end websites — please include links

Must have experience with 3D design and scroll-based animations

Experience with interactive or demo-driven websites a big plus

Experience with Retell AI or ElevenLabs integration a significant advantage

What timeframe would you need to complete this project?

title: HIRING: VIDEO EDITOR (FULL-TIME)
description:
Hey — I’m Karos. I’m 17, and I’m running a 7-figure e-commerce brand. This is not a corporate, LinkedIn-style job post. No fluff. No buzzwords. No BS. I’m looking for a full-time Video Editor who understands direct response and actually cares about performance. This is not a “just cut clips together” role. This is about making ads that perform.



I’m building fast and I’m obsessed with results. This is only the beginning. If you join now, you’re not just editing videos — you’re growing with me, long-term. This is NOT a short-term gig. It’s a chance to build something real together.



What you’ll be doing:

Editing direct-response video ads (Meta-focused)

Creating high-converting ads, not just aesthetic edits

Producing 3 hooks per video / per concept

Turning our raw ideas + strategy into scroll-stopping creatives

Using AI tools like ElevenLabs and AI Personas where it makes sense

Iterating based on performance data

Working directly with me — fast feedback, fast decisions

Helping push the brand from “doing well” to dominating



Highly valued skills:

- Graphic design experience. A strong eye for statics, headlines, and layouts. Experience making static ads that convert

- AI UGC: Experience with tools like HeyGen, Google Veo, Nano Banana

Candidates with either of these skill sets will have a strong advantage.



Who I’m looking for:

You’ve edited direct-response ads for e-commerce brands

You understand performance, not just visuals

You have a STRONG EYE for details

You’re fast, disciplined, and competitive

You care about results and high standards

You want to work exclusively on one brand and help grow it

If you want something slow, safe, and corporate — this isn’t for you.



Compensation:

Full-time, remote role

Base salary depends on experience and skill

Performance-based bonuses tied directly to ad results

For strong performers, monthly bonuses can realistically reach around $1,000, depending on output and performance. There’s no fixed cap — results drive upside. No guarantees, no rushing. If you make winning ads, you make more money. When the brand wins, you win.



The real opportunity:

This starts as a Video Editor role, but there’s room to grow. If you consistently deliver, understand what’s working, and show good judgment over time, you can move into a better role with more responsibility and higher pay. I’m building brands for the long run and looking for people who want stability, ownership, and multi-year collaboration, not constant job hopping.



Application process (important):

To filter unserious applicants, start your message with:

“I create high-converting creatives.”

Then include:

Your experience with video editing & direct response ads

Whether you have static ad / graphic design experience

Links to relevant e-commerce ad work (portfolio)

Your availability and start date

Why you want to work with us



If this resonates and you’d thrive in this environment, D
 and I’ll get back to you.

Speak soon, Karos

title: Operations & Account Manager (U.S. Security – 24/7 Leadership Role)
description:
Operations & Account Manager (U.S. Security – 24/7 Leadership Role)

Remote (Philippines) | Full-Time | 9:00 AM–5:00 PM PST + On-Call



We operate a 24/7 U.S.-based security services company managing 50+ field employees. We are hiring a leader, not an assistant.



You will own scheduling, coverage control, client communication, and operational stability. If coverage breaks, you fix it. If a client escalates, you take control. If a guard becomes unstable, you address it.



This role requires real operations leadership.



Core Responsibilities

• Manage daily scheduling for 50+ U.S. field employees

• Prevent missed shifts and coverage gaps

• Handle call-offs, replacements, OT control, and shift adjustments

• Enforce SLA response times

• Communicate professionally with U.S. clients

• Maintain strict CRM documentation in GoHighLevel

• Coordinate with dispatch, HR, and finance

• Track attendance, performance issues, and guard stability



Required Experience (Strict)

• 3+ years in operations, dispatch, logistics, or 24/7 service environments

• Experience managing scheduling for 30+ field employees

• Experience handling call-offs and urgent coverage issues

• Experience using GoHighLevel (required)

• Experience with CRM/ticketing systems

• Basic HR experience (attendance, discipline, performance issues)

• Strong English communication (written + verbal)

• Comfortable making decisions without supervision



If you have not managed scheduling at scale, do not apply.



Schedule

• 9:00 AM–5:00 PM PST

• Must be available for urgent operational matters after hours



Compensation

• $6–$10 USD/hour

• Performance-based raises

• Growth path into Senior Operations Lead



Application Requirements (Mandatory)

Submit ALL of the following:

 1. Resume (PDF)

 2. 2-minute video explaining your experience managing 30+ field staff

 3. 60-second voice recording confirming PST + on-call availability

 4. List of systems you’ve used (must include GoHighLevel)



Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.



We are hiring ownership, not task followers.

title: GoHighLevel (GHL) Setup & Sales Operations Specialist
description:
GoHighLevel (GHL) Setup & Sales Operations Specialist



Industry: Promotional Products, Custom Apparel & T-Shirt Design (B2B)



Company: New US-Based Promo & Apparel Brand

Location: Remote (Philippines preferred)

Hours: Full-Time (40 hours/week)

Pay: $5–$9/hour (based on experience)

Contract: 30-day trial ? long-term full-time role based on performance



?



About the Role



We’re launching a new promotional products & custom apparel company (t-shirts, uniforms, branded merchandise) and are hiring a full-time GoHighLevel (GHL) Setup & Sales Operations Specialist. This person will own the initial CRM setup (Phase 1) and then help run day-to-day sales operations as the business scales.



This is a hands-on role for someone who already understands how promo/apparel businesses operate — including quoting workflows, artwork/proof processes, order timelines, and B2B client communication. You’ll work directly with the founder to build a clean, scalable sales engine from day one.



?



Phase 1 Responsibilities (First 30 Days)

 • Set up GoHighLevel CRM from scratch

 • Build sales pipelines for:

 • New Leads

 • Contacted

 • Qualified

 • Quote Sent

 • Follow-Up

 • Closed Won / Closed Lost

 • Create lead intake forms for:

 • Promo products

 • Apparel / t-shirt orders

 • Configure:

 • Tags (promo vs apparel, schools vs businesses, seasons, etc.)

 • Custom fields (quantity, budget, timeline, product type, design needed)

 • Set up basic automated follow-up sequences (SMS/email)

 • Prepare GHL so outbound callers can use it easily (call logs, notes, stage movement)

 • Document basic SOPs for sales workflow



?



Ongoing Responsibilities (After Phase 1)

 • Maintain and improve GHL pipelines and automations

 • Manage lead flow and ensure every lead is followed up properly

 • Support outbound calling operations (updating statuses, tagging, reminders)

 • Help monitor quote follow-ups and deal progress

 • Assist with reactivation campaigns for past customers

 • Maintain clean CRM data and reporting

 • Coordinate with the founder on continuous process improvements



?



Requirements (Must-Have)

 • Hands-on experience setting up and managing GoHighLevel (GHL)

 • Direct experience in promotional products, custom apparel, t-shirt design, print shops, embroidery, or screen printing

 • Understanding of B2B sales workflows (lead ? quote ? follow-up ? close)

 • Strong written and spoken English

 • Highly organized, detail-oriented, process-driven

 • Reliable internet connection and professional remote work setup

 • Ability to work U.S. business hours (or partial overlap)



?



Nice to Have (Strong Plus)

 • Experience supporting outbound sales or appointment setting teams

 • Familiarity with quoting tools or artwork/proof workflows

 • Basic understanding of Google Ads lead handling or CRM integrations

 • Experience creating simple SOPs or training materials for sales teams



?



What Success Looks Like (90-Day Outcomes)

 • GHL fully set up and cleanly organized

 • Every lead is properly tagged, tracked, and followed up

 • Quote follow-ups are automated and no deals fall through the cracks

 • Outbound team can operate smoothly inside GHL

 • Founder has clear visibility into pipeline, deals, and revenue



?



How to Apply



Please include:

 1. A short description of your experience with GoHighLevel

 2. Your experience in promo/apparel / print / t-shirt businesses

 3. Examples of CRMs or sales pipelines you’ve set up

 4. Your availability and preferred working hours

 5. Your expected hourly rate

 Management: 95 percent of non-urgent

title: Operations Controller / Dispatch Manager – Security Services (Leadership Role)
description:
Operations Controller / Dispatch Manager – Security Services (Leadership Role)



?



Job Overview



This is NOT a virtual assistant role.



This is a leadership position inside a live operations environment where you will be responsible for controlling active security services, managing guard coverage, handling client issues, and making real-time decisions.



You are not here to “assist.”

You are here to own outcomes and maintain control of operations.



If you are not comfortable taking responsibility, making decisions under pressure, and being held accountable — do not apply.



?



What You Will Be Responsible For

 • Owning guard coverage across multiple sites (scheduling, call-offs, replacements)

 • Handling real-time operational issues (no-shows, late guards, client complaints)

 • Leading communication with clients (phone +
) with confidence and control

 • Managing and updating all tickets, tasks, and issues (CRM systems)

 • Ensuring nothing is missed, delayed, or ignored

 • Coordinating across departments to resolve problems quickly

 • Escalating critical issues appropriately



This is a control tower role — you are responsible for keeping operations stable.



?



Leadership Expectations

 • You take ownership of problems — not pass them to others

 • You make decisions without waiting for instructions

 • You stay calm and in control during high-pressure situations

 • You think ahead and prevent issues before they happen

 • You communicate clearly, directly, and professionally



You are expected to operate like a manager, not an assistant.



?



Work Schedule

 • 45 hours per week (full-time)

 • Must be available during U.S. business hours

 • On-call availability for urgent issues (24/7 responsibility mindset required)



This role requires someone who understands that operations do not stop.



?



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)



You must have experience in at least ONE of the following:

 • Dispatch or logistics coordination

 • Property management coordination

 • Field service scheduling

 • Call center TEAM LEAD (not agent)



If you do NOT have this experience, do not apply.



?



Compensation

 • $10–$13 per hour (based on experience)

 • Performance bonuses tied to operational performance

 • Long-term leadership opportunity



This is not an entry-level role.



?



APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS (MANDATORY)



You MUST answer all questions below:



1. Describe a real situation where operations broke down (missed shift, angry client, scheduling failure). What did you do step-by-step?



2. A guard calls off 1 hour before shift. The client is already frustrated. No replacement is immediately available. What do you do?



3. What systems have you used for scheduling, dispatch, or CRM?



4. Why should we trust you to handle a high-responsibility operations role?



Applications without answers will be rejected.



?



IMPORTANT



We are NOT looking for:

 • Virtual assistants

 • Data entry staff

 • Basic customer service agents



We are looking for someone who can lead operations, solve problems, and take ownership.



?





Include the word “CONTROL” at the top of your application to confirm you read this.

title: YouTube & Instagram Reels Editor
description:
YouTube Video Editor — Weekly Lifestyle & Beauty Content (1 YT Video Week & 5 Reels Per Week) 



Hello!

My name is Ayanna, and I’m looking for a reliable, talented YouTube video editor to join my team long-term. I run a lifestyle, beauty, and wellness YouTube channel & Instagram. These videos include:

 • Vlogs & multiple montage cuts

 • Sit-down videos

 • Chatty videos

 • Hauls

 • Beauty routines

 • Travel montages

 • Lifestyle montages

 • Story-time style edits



I need someone who understands fast-paced, modern editing and can turn raw footage into clean, engaging, binge-able content.







???? What You’ll Be Doing

 • Editing one (1) YouTube video per week, consistently.

 • Taking 45–90 minutes of raw footage and editing it down into a polished 20–30 minute final video.

 • Editing vlogs, sit-downs, and montage-style sections with smooth pacing, transitions, captions (when needed), subtle sound design, and simple effects.

 • Adding music (royalty-free), light color correction, and minor audio cleanup. ( I’ll send over music)

 • Creating simple & aesthetic thumbnails (1 per video).

 • Delivering files through Google Drive (final video + thumbnail).

 • Participating in occasional check-ins for feedback and improvement.



Instagram Reels

* Fast pace 30-60s Videos

*2 clips per second (clip being 0.5 of a second)

*Need to be aesthetic 

*Quick Vlogs 

*Need to Capture THE BEST Footage

*Needs an eye for content 

*you will be receiving video clips & Audio to combine together





???? Skills I’m Looking For

 • Strong proficiency in video editing software (Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, CapCut Desktop, or DaVinci Resolve).

 • Experience editing lifestyle vlogs and beauty content — bonus if you’ve edited for influencers before.

 • Ability to keep pacing engaging and aesthetic.

 • Basic graphic design skills for thumbnail creation.

 • Ability to meet deadlines and communicate clearly.

 • Must handle consistent weekly output.







???? Pay

 • $500 USD per month

 • Paid monthly and consistently

 • Long-term role (6+ months minimum commitment)







???? Requirements

 • At least 1–2 years of editing experience

 • Portfolio or sample edits (You must attach examples of vlog or sit-down editing)

 • Stable WiFi + consistent availability

 • Willing to use Google Drive for all file exchanges







???? Bonus Skills (Not Required, But Preferred)

 • Knowledge of YouTube pacing and retention

 • Ability to work independently without micromanaging







???? How to Apply



Please send:

“Do donkeys exist?”



^^PLEASE
 ME THIS 

(Your application will not be considered without this.)







???? Working Style

 • All footage/notes will be shared via Google Drive.

 • Communication mainly through
 or WhatsApp.

 • I prefer editors who are proactive, creative, and can match my brand’s vibe.







If you can consistently deliver clean, engaging, aesthetic YouTube videos and stick to a weekly posting schedule, I would love to work with you.

Tools:

We are NOT looking for:

We are looking for someone long-term. If you perform, you will grow with us.

title: Virtual Assistant
description:
# Executive Virtual Assistant



**Company:** A hospitality and technology holding company

**Location:** Remote (Philippines preferred)

**Type:** Full-time

**Compensation:** $800 to $1,000/month USD



---



## About Us



We are a bootstrapped holding company with businesses across India, Thailand, and the UAE. The portfolio covers specialty coffee, boutique hospitality, and AI-powered SaaS products. We are founder-led, we move fast, and we are in the middle of a serious growth phase.



---



## About the Role



We need someone who can be the founder's operational right hand. Not a traditional VA who sits around waiting for tasks. The person in this role keeps the entire operation moving. You track tasks across multiple teams, you follow up relentlessly, you consolidate reports from our physical locations, you support our fundraising process, and you manage calendar and travel across three countries.



If something is overdue, you catch it before anyone has to ask. If someone has not delivered, you are already chasing them. If there is a blocker, you bring it up with enough context that a decision can be made in minutes.



---



## What You Will Do



### Task Tracking and Follow-Ups (This is the main job)

- Own the daily and weekly follow-up cycle across all teams and contractors

- Maintain a central task tracker with clear statuses. On track, blocked, overdue, completed.

- Chase people for updates. Proactively, not when asked.

- Send a daily end-of-day summary to the founder. What moved, what is stuck, what needs attention tomorrow.

- Deliver a weekly rollup covering progress, open items, and deadlines across all businesses.



### Operations Reporting

- Collect daily and weekly reports from multiple store locations across three countries

- Consolidate everything into one central dashboard. Revenue, footfall, inventory flags, staffing issues, anything notable.

- Flag anomalies and trends before they become problems

- Chase store managers for reports when they are late



### Fundraising and Investor Support

- Maintain and update an investor CRM with outreach status, follow-up dates, and notes

- Draft outreach
s and messages using templates the founder provides

- Schedule investor calls across multiple time zones

- Follow up after meetings with thank you notes, document sharing, and next steps

- Research investors and prepare briefing materials before calls



### Calendar, Travel, and Coordination

- Manage the founder's calendar across India, Thailand, and UAE

- Book flights, hotels, and transport for frequent international travel

- Coordinate with vendors, landlords, and contractors. Confir
etings, chase invoices, follow up on deliverables.

- Handle visa and documentation logistics



### Research and Admin

- Pull together market data, competitor info, and benchmarks when needed

- Research tools, platforms, and service providers

- Manage inbox triage. Flag what matters, archive the noise, draft responses where you can.

- Keep shared drives and folders organized



---



## What This Role Is Not



You will not be making strategic decisions. You make sure decisions actually get executed. You will not be managing people. You track whether people are delivering. You will not be creating marketing content or analyzing marketing data. Those are separate roles on the team.



---



## Tools



Google Workspace (Calendar, Sheets, Docs, Gmail), Notion or Asana, WhatsApp, Slack, lightweight CRM tools, travel booking platforms. Using AI tools like ChatGPT or Claude for drafting and research is encouraged.



---



## What We Are Looking For



**Must have:**

- At least 2 years as a VA or executive assistant, ideally working with a startup founder or small business owner

- Exceptional written and spoken English

- Comfortable working across multiple time zones (India, Southeast Asia, Middle East)

- Highly organized with a natural bias for action. You chase, you do not wait.

- Strong with spreadsheets. You can build and maintain trackers and dashboards without hand-holding.

- Experience with Google Workspace and project management tools



**Good to have:**

- Background in F&B, hospitality, or SaaS

- Experience supporting fundraising or investor relations

- Experience working with distributed teams across multiple countries



---



## How We Measure Success



- Daily EOD summaries sent every working day without fail

- Every task tracked and followed up within 24 hours

- Store reports consolidated daily by a set deadline

- Zero double-bookings on the founder's calendar

- Only real blockers get escalated. No noise.

- Weekly rollup delivered every Friday or Saturday



---



## First 30 Days



**Week 1:** Onboarding. Understand all the businesses, meet key tea
mbers and store managers, get access to everything, shadow the founder's workflow.



**Week 2:** Take over calendar, inbox triage. Start building the task tracker and store reporting template.



**Week 3:** Own the daily follow-up cycle. Consolidate first full week of store reports. Begin investor outreach support.



**Week 4:** Full ownership. Daily summaries, store dashboard, investor CRM, first weekly rollup delivered.

title: Content Editor and Operator
description:
# Content Editor and Operator



**Company:** A hospitality and technology holding company

**Location:** Remote

**Type:** Full-time

**Compensation:** $900 to $1,100/month USD



---



## About Us



We are a bootstrapped holding company with businesses across India, Thailand, and the UAE. The portfolio covers specialty coffee, boutique hospitality, and AI-powered SaaS products. Six brands, each with its own audience, voice, and platform strategy. We are scaling our content output significantly and need someone who can keep up.



---



## About the Role



We need a content operator who can do two things really well.



First, atomization. The founder regularly writes long-form content. Articles, threads, essays, video scripts. Your job is to take that material and break it into dozens of platform-native social media posts. Each one tailored to the right brand, the right platform, the right audience.



Second, original creation. You will get content ideas in proper formats. No one-line concept. No voice note. Sometimes a rough brief or a reference post but mostly a proper brief. You take that and turn it into a fully finished creative. Copy, visual direction, hashtags, CTA. Ready to schedule.



This is not a proofreading job. You need to understand that what works on LinkedIn does not work on Instagram. You need to match six different brand voices without mixing them up. And you need to turn one good idea into ten pieces of content without losing what made it good in the first place.



---



## What You Will Do



### Content Atomization (50% of your time)

- Take the founder's long-form content (articles, Substack posts, video transcripts, thread drafts, podcast notes) and break it into platform-native posts

- Adapt tone, length, and format per platform. LinkedIn posts, Instagram captions, X threads,
 posts, carousel outlines, reel scripts.

- Pull out quotable lines, stats, hot takes, and standalone insights

- Add each piece to the content calendar with a suggested publish date

- Make sure no two platforms get the same post. Everything should feel native to where it lives.



Typical output from one long-form piece:

- 2 to 3 LinkedIn posts (short-form and carousel outlines)

- 2 to 3 Instagram posts (caption plus visual direction)

- 1 to 2 X threads or standalone tweets

- 1
 post where relevant

- 1 Substack teaser or cross-promo if applicable



### Original Content Creation (40% of your time)

- Get a content idea from the founder and build the full creative. Headline, hook, body copy, CTA, hashtags, visual direction.

- Match the right brand voice for each piece

- Suggest the best platform and format

- For visual posts, write a clear creative brief or image prompt

- For carousels, write all slides with a clear hierarchy

- For reels and short video, write scripts with hook, body, CTA, and visual treatment notes



### Content Pipeline (10% of your time)

- Maintain the content calendar across all six brands

- Keep the posting cadence consistent. Target is 50+ posts per week across all brands combined.

- Flag gaps in the calendar proactively and suggest filler or repurposing ideas

- Quality check everything before it goes out. No typos, correct handles and hashtags, links work, voice is right.



---



## The Brands



You will write for six brands spanning specialty coffee, boutique hotels and bars, AI SaaS, hospitality technology, a founder's personal brand, and a consumer app. Each one has a different voice:



- **Specialty Coffee (India, UAE):** Warm, craft-forward, premium but approachable

- **Boutique Hospitality (Thailand):** Adventurous, laid-back, experiential

- **AI SaaS:** Sharp, technical but accessible, thought-leadership

- **Hospitality SaaS:** Professional, solution-oriented

- **Founder Brand:** Bold, entrepreneurial, builder energy

- **Consumer App:** Emotional, relatable, playful, Gen-Z and millennial



Full brand guidelines and past content will be shared during onboarding.



---



## Content Standards



These apply to everything you write:



- No em dashes. Use commas, periods, or restructure the sentence.

- No AI-sounding language. If you catch yourself writing "delve", "landscape", "leverage", "utilize", or "harness", delete it and try again.

- Clean, punchy prose. Short sentences. Strong verbs.

- Write like a human, not a brand playbook.

- All drafts delivered in markdown format.



---



## What This Role Is Not



You are not managing ad spend or paid campaigns. You are not doing graphic design, though you will direct visuals. You are not writing investor decks. You are not analyzing platfor
trics or building dashboards. A separate Growth Analyst handles that. You are not managing comments or DMs.



---



## Tools



Google Sheets or Notion (content calendar), Google Docs or Obsidian (writing), Buffer or Later or SocialBee (scheduling, TBD), Canva (basic creatives), Midjourney or similar (image prompts), WhatsApp, Slack,
. Using AI tools for drafting and ideation is fine and encouraged, but every output must be human-edited and voice-matched before you hand it over.



---



## What We Are Looking For



**Must have:**

- At least 2 years creating social media content, ideally for multiple brands at once

- Strong English copywriting. You write punchy, scroll-stopping social copy.

- You understand platform-native content. What works on LinkedIn, Instagram, X,
, and TikTok are all different and you know why.

- Experience breaking long-form content into social posts (atomization or repurposing workflows)

- Comfortable working from rough briefs, voice notes, and half-formed ideas

- Basic visual sense. You can put together a clean Canva graphic or write a solid image prompt.



**Good to have:**

- Experience in F&B, hospitality, or tech/SaaS content

- Familiarity with Obsidian, markdown workflows, or AI-assisted content pipelines

- Experience writing carousel scripts and short-form video scripts



---



## How We Measure Success



- 30+ posts delivered per week across all brands

- Atomized content turned around within 48 hours of receiving the long-form input

- Original posts delivered within 24 hours of receiving a brief

- Voice accuracy is high enough that the founder only needs light edits, not rewrites

- No brand goes more than 2 days without a scheduled post



---



## First 30 Days



**Week 1:** Onboarding. Study all six brands. Read the last 3 months of content. Understand the founder's voice. Review brand guidelines.



**Week 2:** Shadow the current process. Atomize 2 to 3 existing long-form pieces as a test. Get feedback on voice and platform fit.



**Week 3:** Take over daily atomization. Start creating original posts from briefs. Set up or take ownership of the content calendar.



**Week 4:** Full ownership. The founder gives you ideas and long-form. You handle everything from there.

title: Growth Analyst and Marketing Ops
description:
# Growth Analyst and Marketing Ops



**Company:** A hospitality and technology holding company

**Location:** Remote

**Type:** Full-time

**Compensation:** $900 to $1,200/month USD



---



## About Us



We are a bootstrapped holding company with businesses across India, Thailand, and the UAE. The portfolio covers specialty coffee, boutique hospitality, and AI-powered SaaS products. Six brands, physical locations in multiple countries, and growing digital presences across all of them. We are in an active growth phase and need someone who can own the marketing data layer end to end.



---



## About the Role



We need someone who can look at our marketing data across six brands and tell us what is working, what is not, and what to do about it. Not just dashboards. Not just numbers in a spreadsheet. Actual insight. "Here is what happened, here is what it means, here is what we should change."



You will also own influencer outreach across all brands and markets. Finding the right people, reaching out, managing the relationship, and tracking whether the collaboration actually moved the needle.



If you are the kind of person who sees a metric and immediately thinks "okay but why, and what do we do now?", this is your role.



---



## What You Will Do



### Marketing Analytics and Reporting (40% of your time)



**Weekly Dashboard (every Monday)**



A consolidated view across all six brands covering:



**Social Media (Instagram, LinkedIn, X,
, TikTok)**

Follower growth, engagement rates, top performing posts, reach and impressions trends, which content formats are winning.



**Google Business and Maps**

Profile views, search appearances, direction requests, review count and ratings across all physical locations.



**Website Analytics (GA4 and Google Search Console)**

Sessions, users, bounce rate, top landing pages, traffic sources, keyword rankings, conversion events.



**Ad Platforms (Meta Ads, Google Ads)**

Spend, impressions, clicks, CTR, CPC, conversions, ROAS per campaign. Clear recommendations on what to pause, scale, or adjust.



**Monthly Deep Dive (by the 5th of each month)**



Month-over-month trends. What worked and why. What underperformed and your best guess at why. 3 to 5 specific recommendations for the next month. Competitive benchmarks where you can find them.



The founder does not want a data dump. He wants a briefing he can read in 5 minutes that makes it obvious what to do next.



### Influencer Outreach and Partnerships (30% of your time) - A Marketing strategists leads this, you are in a support role.



**Finding the right people**

- Build and maintain an influencer database by brand and market (India, Thailand, UAE)

- Focus on micro (5K to 50K) and mid-tier (50K to 200K) influencers

- Evaluate based on audience overlap, engagement rate, content quality, and brand alignment. Not just follower count.

- Prioritize by relevance. Food and coffee creators for the cafe brands. Travel and hospitality for the hotel brand. Tech and AI for the SaaS products. Couples and relationship content for the consumer app.



**Running the outreach**

- Send initial outreach via DM and
 using approved templates

- Negotiate terms. Barter (complimentary stays, products, access) or paid, depending on the situation.

- Manage the full workflow from outreach to agreement to content coordination to posting to follow-up

- Keep a central tracker with every detail



**Tracking results**

- Track ROI on every collaboration. Reach, engagement, follower lift, website traffic, promo code usage.

- Score influencers and flag who is worth repeating and who is not



### Paid Ads Support (20% of your time) - Founder leads this, you are in a support role.



- Run day-to-day Meta Ads and Google Ads campaigns across the brands

- Set up campaigns, ad sets, and creatives based on the founder's direction

- Monitor daily and make tactical calls. Budget shifts, audience tweaks, creative rotation.

- A/B test creatives and copy, coordinating with the Content Editor for assets

- Give clear spend recommendations with data behind them

- This part of the role will grow over time as budgets increase



### Strategic Insights (10% of your time)



- Surface growth opportunities proactively. Come with data, not just opinions.

- Spot content trends across markets and platforms

- Recommend experiments. Should we try a new platform? Double down on a format? Test a new audience?

- Feed insights to the Content Editor so content decisions are backed by numbers, not just gut.



---



## How This Role Fits the Team



You work alongside two other people:



**Content Editor** creates all content. You tell them what is working and what to make more of. You give them briefs for influencer collab content. You share performance data so they can adjust the content mix.



**Executive VA** handles logistics. If an influencer needs a complimentary hotel stay, the VA books it. If a store needs to prep for a visit, the VA chases the store manager.



**Founder** sets the strategy. You bring the data and recommendations that help him make better decisions.



---



## What This Role Is Not



You are not creating content. The Content Editor does that. You are not managing operations or chasing internal tasks. The VA does that. You are not making final budget or strategy calls. You recommend, the founder d
. You are not a full-time performance marketer yet. Ads are part of the role, not the whole role.



---



## Tools



Meta Business Suite, LinkedIn Analytics, X Analytics, TikTok Analytics, Google Analytics 4, Google Search Console, Google Business Profile Manager, Meta Ads Manager, Google Ads, Google Sheets (primary reporting), Looker Studio (nice to have), WhatsApp, Slack,
. Using AI tools for research and analysis is encouraged.



---



## What We Are Looking For



**Must have:**

- At least 2 years in digital marketing analytics, growth marketing, or social media management

- Comfortable working in GA4, Meta Business Suite, Google Ads, and Search Console

- Can build clean dashboards in Google Sheets or Looker Studio that people actually want to look at

- Experience with influencer marketing. Finding them, reaching out, negotiating, and tracking results.

- You translate numbers into "so what?" and "now what?", not just "here is the data"

- Basic to intermediate experience running Meta Ads and/or Google Ads

- Strong written English



**Good to have:**

- Background in F&B, hospitality, or SaaS marketing

- SEO and content marketing analytics experience

- Experience with influencer tools like Modash, HypeAuditor, or Upfluence

- Experience managing data across multiple brands or markets at once



---



## How We Measure Success



- Weekly dashboard delivered every Monday by a set deadline

- Monthly deep dive delivered by the 5th

- At least 20 new influencer outreach messages per week across all brands

- At least 4 active influencer collaborations per month by month 3

- Every weekly report includes at least 3 specific, actionable recommendations

- Ad performance included in the weekly dashboard



---



## First 30 Days



**Week 1:** Onboarding. Get access to every analytics platform across all six brands. Audit the tracking setup (GA4, pixels, UTMs). Understand where each brand stands.



**Week 2:** Build the first version of the weekly dashboard. Deliver the first Monday report, even if the data is not complete yet. Start influencer research for the two highest-priority brands.



**Week 3:** First complete weekly dashboard. First batch of influencer outreach (at least 10 messages). Audit any existing ad campaigns and share initial recommendations.



**Week 4:** First monthly deep dive. At least 2 influencer conversations in progress. Present a 90-day growth roadmap to the founder covering quick wins and longer bets.

• Weekly YouTube upload

-Optimize posting time

What We Need: Strong knowledge of social media platforms, scheduling and automation tools, video editing software, AI productivity tools, and blogging platforms. Real estate market understanding is essential, or ability to quickly develop fluency by studying successful agent content.

The Right Fit: Self-directed, detail-oriented, comfortable working independently in a fast-paced environment. This is a long-term, stable position.

Success Metrics — 30, 60, 90 Day Plan: Follower growth. Listing video views increasing monthly. Private inquiries tracked and reported weekly.CKPIs (How You’ll Be Measured) Content Performance — average video views per post, Reel plays, and YouTube watch time increasing month over month.

Engagement Rate — likes, comments, shares, and saves as a percentage of followers. A healthy target for real estate is two to three percent.

Google Business — profile views, click-through rates, and direction requests growing monthly.

Lead Generation — number of direct messages, profile link clicks, and contact form submissions tracked weekly.

Content Consistency — hitting the posting schedule with zero missed weeks, maintaining the four-post weekly cadence.

Paid Ad Performance — cost per click and cost per lead decreasing as campaigns are optimized over time.

YouTube — subscriber growth and average watch duration improving monthly.

To Apply: Please send your resume and a portfolio with social media content and videos you have created.

title: Short-Form Video Editor (CapCut)
description:
Paid Trial To Complete (Required to Apply, $150 if you complete it!)

When submitting your application, include these 3 trial videos: 
tion.site/Editor-Application-Test-Brief-326f
ede1d820b810?source=copy_link



Applications without the trial will not be considered.



Role Overview



We are looking for a highly creative and results-driven Performance Video Editor to join our team. Your mission will be to create high-performing organic video for Instagram and TikTok that don't look like ads. If you have a knack for creating content that stops the scroll and engages audiences, we want to talk to you.



This is a long-term, fully remote position for an editor who is passionate about the intersection of creativity and data, and is excited by the opportunity to have their work directly impact business results.



What You'll Do:



- Edit raw footage into compelling, fast-paced short-form video tailored for organic TikToks and Instagram Reels.

- Incorporate viral trends, engaging hooks, and retention-focused pacing to maximize performance.

- Add dynamic captions, relevant B-roll, and sound design to create a native viewing experience.

- Collaborate with our marketing team to test new creative concepts and iterate on winning ads to scale campaigns.

- Analyze ad performance to understand what resonates with the audience and apply those insights to future edits.



Who We're Looking For:



- A Viral Content Creator: You have proven experience creating organic short-form videos (TikToks, Reels) that have achieved significant reach and engagement. You understand what makes content shareable and how to capture attention in the first three seconds.

- A Performance-Minded Editor: You are excited by the idea of your creative work driving measurable results. Experience editing direct-response or paid social ads is a major plus.

- CapCut or Premiere Pro Whiz: You are highly proficient in a fast-paced editing workflow using modern tools.

- Excellent Communicator: You have strong English skills and are reliable, detail-oriented, and can work independently.

- Available & Ready: You have a perfect internet connection and are available to dedicate 3-4 hours daily to this role.

- This is a part-time remote role requiring approximately 24 hours per week (around 4 hours per day, Monday to Saturday), with the potential to grow into a full-time position based on performance.



Compensation & Growth: Earn Based on Performance



We believe in rewarding top talent for outstanding results. This role offers a unique compensation structure designed to give you significant earning potential.



- Base Rate: You will start with a competitive base rate of $10 per hour.

- Performance Bonuses: On top of your hourly rate, you will earn substantial monthly bonuses for creating viral videos and "winning" ads that meet or exceed performance targets. Our bonus structure is uncapped.

- Earning Potential: Our top-performing editors consistently earn $3,000 - $5,000+ per month by creating ads that convert.



This is not a gig. It is a long-term partnership. Most of our editors have been with us for over a year, and we are committed to investing in your growth and success.



Why Join Our Team?



- Fully Remote: Work from anywhere in the world.

- Flexible Schedule: Manage your own hours and workflow.

- Direct Impact: See your creative work translate directly into business success and your own earnings.

- Growth & Stability: Join a team that values long-term relationships and provides ongoing training and opportunities.

title: Video Editor / Creative Strategist (Performance-Based Pay + Free Courses Included)
description:
About Us

We're a performance creative agency that produces direct-response video ads for US-based health and supplement brands. Our clients spend real budgets on the ads we make — which means when your work performs, you earn more.

We're looking for a video editor who wants to grow into a Creative Strategist — someone who doesn't just cut footage, but learns to think about why an ad works and builds the instinct to create ads that sell.



The Role

You'll start by editing short-form video ads (UGC-style, direct response) using scripts, AI tools, and briefs we provide. Over time, you'll develop into a full Creative Strategist — learning how to research markets, develop angles, and build ad concepts from scratch.



Schedule & Time Zone

We don't care when you work — as long as your daily output is delivered and you make the Saturday strategy call. You manage your own hours.

Daily output: 1 ad concept with 3 video variations.



You'll use AI and structured prompts for scripting and iteration, upload deliverables to Google Drive, log everything in our tracking sheet, and join weekly strategy calls. This is a system — you follow the process, use the frameworks, hit your output, and you earn.



How You Get Paid

You earn 2.5% of the first $10,000 in revenue generated by each ad concept you create.

That's up to $250 per winning concept.

At 5+ concepts per week, you're getting 20+ shots on goal every month. You don't need every concept to win. You need a few. And as your skill improves, your hit rate goes up — and so does your income.

There is no monthly cap. The more winners you produce, the more you make.

Revenue is tracked transparently in a shared sheet and payments are made bi-weekly.



What You Get Beyond Pay

Access to premium direct-response and copywriting courses (valued at thousands of dollars) — included free as part of the role

Hands-on training in performance creative strategy — the same skillset US agencies charge $5K–$15K/month for

AI-powered workflow — you'll work with cutting-edge AI tools for scripting, ideation, and review

A real path to growth — you're not stuck editing clips forever. You're building a skillset that compounds



Most editing roles pay you to stay in the same place. This one pays you to get better. You'll understand how ads are built, why they work, and how to create them from scratch. That knowledge compounds whether you stay with us long-term or go on to build something of your own.



This Role Is For You If…

You can edit video cleanly and quickly (Premiere, DaVinci, CapCut — whatever you're fast in)

You want to learn direct-response marketing, not just push pixels

You're reliable, consistent, and can hit a daily output target

You're excited by the idea of getting paid based on results, not hours

You want a long-term role where your earning potential grows as you grow



This Role Is NOT For You If…

You want a fixed salary with no performance component

You're not comfortable with a learning curve

You can't commit to consistent daily output



Final Notes

This is a performance culture.

You get rewarded for speed, output, and results.

If you want to make more, produce more, test more, win more.

The system is here to make sure your pay scales with your impact.



To Apply

Fill out this short form below and link any past work (ads, reels, edits — anything that shows you can cut video).
